title: Canopy Platform Registry
description: Defines supported MCP client platforms and their configuration format for canopy setup wizard.

config_path.description: Path to MCP config file relative to $HOME

config_format.description: Config file format. Defaults to JSON when omitted. Use 'toml' for platforms like Codex.

mcp_servers_key.description: JSON/TOML key path to the MCP servers object (e.g., ['mcpServers'])

canopy_entry_key.description: Key name for the canopy entry within the MCP servers object (e.g., 'canopy')

canopy_entry.description: The canopy MCP server entry value in the platform's expected format

deprecated_keys.description: Old MCP server keys to remove during setup (e.g. task-trigger)

unsupported_keys.description: MCP server config keys that this platform does not support (used to sanitize configs during sync)

cli.description: CLI strategy definition for headless execution

headless_mode.description: Command flags to run in headless mode (before prompt)

interactive_args.description: Arguments to pass when launching in interactive (TUI) mode (e.g., --tui, -c)

fallback_interactive_args.description: Fallback arguments if the primary interactive mode fails to start

resume_args.description: Arguments to resume the most recent session (e.g., --continue, --resume)

session_list_cmd.description: Subcommand to list sessions (e.g., 'session list'). Enables canopy-side session picker.

session_resume_cmd.description: Flag to resume a specific session by ID (e.g., '--session'). The session ID is appended as the next argument.

supports_working_dir.description: Whether this CLI supports working directory flag

env_vars.description: Environment variables to set when running this CLI

accent_color.description: RGB accent color for this CLI's agents in the TUI (e.g., [139, 92, 246])
